channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Federal budget funding for Canberra roads could increase peak-hour traffic
item.description: The latest federal budget has committed $50 million to duplicate a road in West Belconnen, but transport planning experts warn it could encourage even more peak-hour traffic.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Gore and Harry Frost

media:group.media:description: William Hovell Drive is one of three Canberra roads currently slated for duplication work.

item.title: Research links seismic testing to decreased commercial fish catch
item.description: New research links seismic testing used for oil and gas exploration with decreases in commercial fish catch rates in Victoria's east.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Pope

media:group.media:description: Air guns towed by a survey vessel produce loud noises that can be used to detect oil and gas below the sea floor.

item.title: Giro crash hands young Aussie 'Bradbury moment' for best stage finish
item.description: Jensen Plowright never expected to be contesting for stage victories at this year's Giro d'Italia. But the withdrawal of Kaden Groves and yet another calamitous crash gave him his first chance on stage six.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

media:group.media:description: Elmar Reinders appeared to hit the deck first in a relatively low-speed crash with 400 metres to go.

item.title: Archaeologists restoring oldest colonial-era boat ever found in Australia
item.description: Maritime archaeologists are working to restore the oldest colonial-era boat ever found in Australia, discovered during the construction of the Barangaroo metro station in 2018.
item.dc:creator: Julia André

media:group.media:description: The remnants of a colonial-era boat were discovered during construction of the Barangaroo metro station in Sydney.

item.title: 'Different doctor every fortnight' is the reality for these residents
item.description: Jamie hopes his small town's almost decade-long run without a permanent GP is about to end, so he will not have to keep repeating his story to every doctor who passes through.
item.dc:creator: Sandy Powell

media:group.media:description: Strahan residents Jamie and Gaylene McGuinness have not had a regular GP since 2017.

item.title: Community decries government 'arrogance' over fracking bill
item.description: Producers on South Australia's Limestone Coast say they are stunned by a government proposal to lift the fracking ban in the region.
item.dc:creator: Josh Brine, Liz Rymill, and Selina Green

media:group.media:description: Anti-fracking protesters at a country cabinet meeting in Mount Gambier last night.

item.title: 'Mega' gas plant planned to feed hungry AI-data centres
item.description: Energy Australia says the proposed 1.4-gigawatt gas-fired power plant would be critical to the energy transition by supplying reliable back-up power, but analysts have flagged supply and economic issues.
item.dc:creator: Penny Burfitt

media:group.media:description: Energy Australia's 1.4-gigawatt gas-fired power plant has been proposed for Marulan in the Southern Highlands.

item.title: Abandoned base's toxic waste clean-up deadline rejected
item.description: The Australian government has officially ruled out cleaning up Antarctica's contaminated Wilkes Station before the end of the decade, as previously recommended by a federal parliamentary committee.
item.dc:creator: Jano Gibson

media:group.media:description: It was recommended the Australian government fully remediate Wilkes Station by 2030.

item.title: ABC news quiz: A Eurovision boycott, a pungent frog and a mysterious red globule
item.description: This is not the week to boycott the news quiz, when a particularly ripe frog and a big, red … something … await you.

media:group.media:description: Presenters Victoria Swarovski, left, and Michael Ostrowski introduce a light-hearted musical interlude featuring Austria and Australia, as they wait for the results of voting during the first semifinal of the 70th Eurovision Song Contest in Vienna, Austria, Tuesday, May 12, 2026. (AP Photo/Martin Meissner)

item.title: The Liberals said John was dead, but he's considering a new political life
item.description: When John Ternel quit the Victorian Liberal Party this week the party mistakenly sent an email to members saying he had died. Mr Ternel says the ordeal confirmed to him that the Victorian Liberals are in shambles.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Grieve

media:group.media:description: John Ternel has quit the Liberal Party and is considering joining One Nation.

item.title: Magic Round crowds told to pack raincoats ahead of patchy Queensland showers
item.description: A trough in the west and onshore winds in the east will bring showers to parts of Queensland, however the driest parts are set to miss out.
item.dc:creator: Dan McCray

media:group.media:description: Showers are forecast for much of Queensland's east and west this weekend.

item.title: It's been 20 years since the wrong Guy was interviewed by the BBC
item.description: Guy Gomo reflects on his infamous live TV mix-up, which has been immortalised as one of the greatest TV blunders of all time.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Nixon

media:group.media:description: Guy Goma realising what has happened.

item.title: Chinese Australians say Coalition pitch to cut non-citizens' welfare 'sounds like One Nation'
item.description: Chinese Australians have lashed the Coalition's plan to block non-citizens from accessing welfare services, likening the opposition's targeting of migrants to One Nation.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Dick

media:group.media:description: Richard Shi says permanent residents contribute to Australia's economy.

item.title: Tens of thousands of fans descend on Brisbane for blockbuster Magic Round
item.description: Brisbane's Lang Park plays host to the NRL's biggest weekend, with 16 teams competing in eight matches, plus game two of the NRLW State of Origin.
item.dc:creator: Ned Hammond

media:group.media:description: Tens of thousands of fans are expected to turn up for eight contests between 16 teams and the NRLW State of Origin game two.

item.title: Calls for better recognition of Queensland vet technologists and nurses
item.description: Experts says veterinary technologists and nurses are being under-utilised in a sector they say is in "crisis."
item.dc:creator: Niddal Mustafa

media:group.media:description: The veterinary industry wants the range of duties technologists and nurses can perform to be widened.

item.title: Trust tax could hurt young, not just ultra-wealthy, tax advisors warn
item.description: The federal budget includes a new 30 per cent minimum tax on discretionary trusts. Experts say it is not just the ultra-wealthy that will be hit.
item.dc:creator: Nassim Khadem

media:group.media:description: The changes to the taxation of trusts will impact the transfer of intergenerational wealth.

item.title: TasInsure unravelling a lesson for Liberals in promise management
item.description: In a week where "broken promise" is being shouted by the Tasmanian Liberals in support of their federal colleagues' attack on the Labor budget, closer to home, a promise of their own is giving them grief.
item.dc:creator: Josh Duggan

media:group.media:description: Jeremy Rockliff at the TasInsure policy launch in July, 2025.

item.title: How the wisdom of experience won the State of Origin series for New South Wales
item.description: New South Wales series win over Queensland proves that sometimes, like anything else, practice makes perfect in State of Origin football.
item.dc:creator: Nick Campton

media:group.media:description: New South Wales have wrapped up their second-straight State of Origin series win.

item.title: Ambulance data shows overdose callout at Crown Melbourne every two weeks
item.description: Paramedics have attended more than 50 suspected overdoses at Crown Melbourne in the past two years, with the casino complex averaging an ambulance call-out for a suspected overdose every two weeks, data released exclusively to the ABC shows.
item.dc:creator: Elise Kinsella

media:group.media:description: Paramedics were called to more than 50 drug overdoses at Crown Melbourne in 2024 and 2025.

item.title: Finally, a tax fight that actually means something
item.description: Labor and the Liberals are offering imperfect but undeniably bold alternative visions for the tax system. It's nice to have something to talk about.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crowley

media:group.media:description: Angus Taylor and Anthony Albanese outlined duelling visions for the tax system this week.

item.title: Angus Taylor promises permanent tax cuts and to reinstate all negative gearing
item.description: The Coalition would repeal all of Labor's negative gearing and capital gains changes and spend $22.5 billion over four years handing back tax "bracket creep" to workers if elected.
item.dc:creator: Clare Armstrong

media:group.media:description: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor said he would reverse Labor's tax changes if elected.

item.title: Taylor's bold budget reply a mix of sound and dubious policy commitments
item.description: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor commits to phasing in income tax indexation under a "tax back guarantee" that he says "will fully protect all taxpayers from inflation".
item.dc:creator: Michelle Grattan

item.title: Sensory-friendly show breaks 'theatre etiquette' to make audience feel safer
item.description: The State Theatre Company South Australia has introduced a sensory-friendly performance for a broader range of audience members including neurodivergent people.
item.dc:creator: Josephine Lim

media:group.media:description: Alana Tiller says the pre-show tours bring a more complete experience.

item.title: Sam Kerr to leave Chelsea after six years at London club
item.description: The Matildas star has spent over six years playing for the London-based team.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Morgan

media:group.media:description: Chelsea FC praised Sam Kerr for her "sustained success" with the club.

item.title: Food, music and praise served up at the Trump-Xi state banquet
item.description: Guests dined on Beijing roast duck and tiramisu while the People's Liberation Army band performed some classic hits.
item.dc:creator: Claire Campbell

media:group.media:description: Xi Jinping and Donald Trump attend a state banquet at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing.
